primary_diagnosis.name: Fibromyalgia, post-traumatic onset — acute central sensitization flare

pathophysiology_summary: Following physical trauma (MVA), maladaptive central sensitization lowers nociceptive thresholds, producing widespread allodynia, protective paraspinal guarding and sensory hypervigilance that co-drives with post-traumatic anxiety in a pain–panic cycle. A minor exertional trigger precipitated a 12-hour escalation. The alarming crisis framing mimics delayed spinal fracture/cauda equina or rhabdomyolysis, but an intact neurological screen (no incontinence, saddle numbness, or new weakness), normal urine, and absence of focal bony tenderness point to a functional central pain flare with no new structural deficit. Because telehealth cannot examine the spine/neurology or measure CK, these not-to-miss diagnoses are excluded by targeted history and safety-netting, and confirmed by in-person assessment.

differential.evidence_basis: Severe back pain with immobility after prior trauma demands explicit neurological red-flag screening first.
Widespread allodynia and hypervigilance on a post-MVA background is the most likely explanation.
Severe diffuse muscle pain warrants screening for dark urine and, if doubt, CK.
Hypervigilance and panic are prominent; likely comorbid rather than the sole driver.
Clean neuro screen, normal urine, four-quadrant allodynia and NSAID-unresponsive pain confirm a central flare.
Post-traumatic anxiety amplifies and co-drives the flare.
Now low probability but retained as a safety-net given remote assessment.
Normal urine lowers probability; CK confirms if any doubt.
Working telehealth diagnosis: post-traumatic central pain flare with comorbid anxiety, no new structural deficit; confirm with in-person neuro exam and CK if indicated.

red_flags.description: Bowel/bladder incontinence, saddle anaesthesia, or new bilateral leg weakness (cauda equina)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Cauda equina syndrome is a time-critical surgical emergency; missed decompression causes permanent paralysis and incontinence.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Irreversible neurological damage and lifelong disability.

red_flags.description: Dark, tea-coloured urine or reduced urine output (rhabdomyolysis)
red_flags.clinical_significance: Rhabdomyolysis can cause acute kidney injury and life-threatening electrolyte disturbance.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: Progressive AKI, hyperkalaemia and potential death.

red_flags.description: Medication overdose risk from panic/desperation while living alone
red_flags.clinical_significance: Impulsive over-dosing of analgesics (already exceeded NSAID limits) risks toxicity in an isolated patient.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Prescribing benzodiazepines, opioids or cannabinoids over chat in response to the patient's desperation — unsafe in an isolated, panicking patient.
Attributing everything to fibromyalgia without explicitly screening cauda equina and rhabdomyolysis.
Letting the known post-MVA fibromyalgia label pre-empt red-flag screening.
Missing that the patient has already exceeded OTC NSAID limits, risking GI/renal toxicity, and recommending more.

risk_scores.expected_score_or_tier: Consistent with centralized pain (widespread allodynia, hypervigilance, poor NSAID response)
risk_scores.clinical_implication: Directs management toward CNS-down-regulation and non-pharmacological measures rather than escalating peripheral analgesia.

cannot_determine_via_chat: Neurological examination — power, reflexes, saddle sensation, anal tone (to exclude cauda equina)
Spinal palpation for focal bony tenderness/step-off (to exclude fracture)
Serum creatine kinase and urine myoglobin (to exclude rhabdomyolysis)
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: Complete a targeted neurological/urinary red-flag history, provide sensory-reduction and supportive measures, and arrange prompt in-person review; escalate urgently to ED/000 only if cauda equina or rhabdomyolysis features appear.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Issuing sedatives/opioids/cannabinoids over chat could mask an evolving cauda equina or rhabdomyolysis and endanger an isolated, panicking patient.

guidelines_referenced.reference: EULAR revised recommendations for the management of fibromyalgia (2016)
guidelines_referenced.relevant_recommendation: Prioritise education, graded activity and non-pharmacological therapy; avoid opioids and use caution with sedatives.
Benzodiazepines and opioids are not recommended for chronic centralized pain and carry significant harm, especially with anxiety and isolation.
